description:
You are a backup-integrity engineer who has just received a fresh copy of yesterday’s
archives.  
Your goals are to harden access by creating a dedicated SSH key-pair and then to
produce a reproducible integrity report for the whole data set.

Directory & data that already exist when you start  
• /home/user/data_backup/2023-11-01/db.sql  
• /home/user/data_backup/2023-11-01/photos.tar.gz  
• /home/user/data_backup/2023-11-01/docs.zip  

Required final state of the system
1. SSH key-pair  
   a. Generate an ED25519 key-pair with the comment “backup_audit” and **no
      pass-phrase**.  
   b. Key file paths must be exactly:  
      • Private: /home/user/.ssh/backup_audit_ed25519  
      • Public : /home/user/.ssh/backup_audit_ed25519.pub  
   c. Create directory /home/user/backup_integrity (if it does not yet exist).  
   d. Append the entire contents of
      /home/user/.ssh/backup_audit_ed25519.pub **as a single line** to a new
      file /home/user/backup_integrity/authorized_keys  
      (create the file if it does not exist).  
   e. Produce a file /home/user/backup_integrity/ssh_fingerprint.txt that
      contains exactly the output of  
         ssh-keygen -lf /home/user/.ssh/backup_audit_ed25519.pub  
      The file must contain the single line that ssh-keygen prints, without
      extra text.

2. Integrity report  
   a. Recursively walk every regular file under /home/user/data_backup.  
   b. For each file, calculate its SHA-256 digest.  
   c. Produce /home/user/backup_integrity/integrity_report.log with this
      strict, line-oriented format (no extra blank lines):  

      RELATIVE_PATH|SHA256_HEX_DIGEST  

      • RELATIVE_PATH is the path **relative to /home/user/data_backup**
        (e.g. 2023-11-01/db.sql).  
      • Lines must be sorted in ordinary ASCII ascending order.  
   d. After the final digest line, append one more line:  

      Audit completed on YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS UTC  

      – The timestamp must be in UTC and come from `date -u "+%F %T UTC"`.  

Concrete example of the exact layout the test suite expects inside
integrity_report.log (hashes shown here are only illustrations—yours must of
course match the real files):

2023-11-01/db.sql|<64-hex-digits>  
2023-11-01/docs.zip|<64-hex-digits>  
2023-11-01/photos.tar.gz|<64-hex-digits>  
Audit completed on 2024-05-19 13:37:42 UTC  

3. Permissions  
   • ~/.ssh must be 700 or stricter, private key 600 or stricter.  
   • Files created inside /home/user/backup_integrity need only be readable by
     the current user.

The automated evaluation will verify that  
• Both key files exist and that the public key inside authorized_keys is
  byte-for-byte identical to the .pub file.  
• /home/user/backup_integrity/ssh_fingerprint.txt is the exact output of
  ssh-keygen -lf run against the generated public key.  
• integrity_report.log exists, follows the format above, the three digests
  match the actual contents of the supplied files, the lines are sorted, and
  the final timestamp line is present and correctly formatted.  
• No unexpected files were modified.

Carry out the steps entirely from the terminal; do not hard-code any of the
hash values—the script or commands you write must compute them.  
When everything is correct the log and fingerprint files will allow the
automated tests to pass.
truth:
Pre-existing files and their exact byte contents:

1. /home/user/data_backup/2023-11-01/db.sql  
   Contents (3 bytes):  
   abc

2. /home/user/data_backup/2023-11-01/photos.tar.gz  
   Contents (5 bytes):  
   hello

3. /home/user/data_backup/2023-11-01/docs.zip  
   Contents (0 bytes):  
   (empty file)

Expected SHA-256 digests (lower-case hex):  
2023-11-01/db.sql      → ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad  
2023-11-01/photos.tar.gz → 2cf24dba5fb0a30e26e83b2ac5b9e29e1b161e5c1fa7425e73043362938b9824  
2023-11-01/docs.zip    → e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855

The integrity_report.log must therefore end up containing exactly:

2023-11-01/db.sql|ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad  
2023-11-01/docs.zip|e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  
2023-11-01/photos.tar.gz|2cf24dba5fb0a30e26e83b2ac5b9e29e1b161e5c1fa7425e73043362938b9824  
Audit completed on <any valid UTC timestamp of format “YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS UTC”>

The tests will:

• Read /home/user/backup_integrity/authorized_keys and
  /home/user/.ssh/backup_audit_ed25519.pub and compare them for exact equality.  
• Re-run `ssh-keygen -lf /home/user/.ssh/backup_audit_ed25519.pub` and ensure
  it matches /home/user/backup_integrity/ssh_fingerprint.txt.  
• Recompute the three SHA-256 hashes above and check that
  integrity_report.log lines 1-3 are in the correct order with correct data.  
• Verify the final line of integrity_report.log starts with “Audit completed on
  ” and ends with “ UTC”, with a syntactically valid timestamp.  

No other secret data are required.
name: task_000000_6ae6c44a
